tr.td: Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
Cholesterol in HDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated HbA1c, indicating suboptimal long-term glycemic control (diabetes or prediabetes). Creatinine and potassium are mildly elevated, suggesting possible early renal impairment. Total CO₂ is slightly low, which may indicate mild metabolic acidosis. LDL cholesterol is above target, increasing cardiovascular risk. Other parameters are within normal limits. Recommend clinical correlation, review of diabetes and renal management, and cardiovascular risk reduction.
Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
